note: Major recent flood disasters in Europe. Deaths are reported totals; costs are approximate published damage estimates, rounded.

events.name: Western Europe floods
events.detail: Two months of rain in two days. The Ahr valley alone lost 134 people; water flows reached an estimated 100× normal.

events.name: Storm Boris
events.detail: Record rainfall across Austria, Czechia, Poland, Romania and neighbours — the most widespread European flooding in over a decade began here.

events.name: Valencia DANA
events.detail: Chiva received 491 mm of rain in eight hours — roughly a year's worth. 15,000 people were displaced.

attribution2021: Climate change made the 2021 rainfall event 1.2-9× more likely and 3-19% more intense (World Weather Attribution).
